{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2015}} {{Use mdy dates|date=February 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 72 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = 72nd Golden Globe Awards.png | caption = Official poster | date = January 11, 2015 | site = [[The Beverly Hilton]],
[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | host = [[Tina Fey]]
[[Amy Poehler]] | network = [[NBC]] | most_wins = ''Boyhood'' (3) | most_nominations = ''Birdman'' (7) | best_film_drama = ''[[Boyhood (2014 film)|Boyhood]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[The Grand Budapest Hotel]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[The Affair (TV series)|The Affair]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Transparent (TV series)|Transparent]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[Fargo (TV series)|Fargo]]'' | last = 71st | next = 73rd }} The '''72nd [[Golden Globe Award]]s''', honoring the best in [[2014 in film|film]] and [[2014 in American television|American television of 2014]], was broadcast live from the [[Beverly Hilton Hotel]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]] on January 11, 2015, by [[NBC]].Butler, Don. [http://www.canada.com/reginaleaderpost/news/travel/story.html?id=bcf5dd9d-d70e-4f17-a396-3b8e0f65f01c "Beverly Hilton recaptures lustre of its glory days"] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151108012716/http://www.canada.com/reginaleaderpost/news/travel/story.html?id=bcf5dd9d-d70e-4f17-a396-3b8e0f65f01c |date=November 8, 2015}}, ''[[Regina Leader-Post]]'', May 9, 2008. "And the Golden Globe Awards have been handed out in its swanky International Ballroom since 1961." The ceremony was produced by [[Dick Clark Productions]] in association with the [[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]]. [[George Clooney]] was announced as the [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Lifetime Achievement Award]] honoree on September 14, 2014.{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2015/jan/11/golden-globes-2015-live|title=Golden Globes 2015 - as it happened|date=12 January 2015 |work=Guardian |access-date=12 January 2015}}{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/golden-globes/11339036/golden-globes-2015-winners.html|title=Golden Globes 2015 - full list of winners|date=12 January 2015 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=12 January 2015}} [[Tina Fey]] and [[Amy Poehler]] were the co-hosts for the third consecutive time.{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/tina-fey-amy-poehler-host-648283|title=Tina Fey, Amy Poehler to Host Golden Globes for Next Two Years|access-date=2014-12-09|work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]|author=Belloni, Matthew|date=15 October 2013 }}[https://time.com/3544249/golden-globes-tina-fey-amy-poehler/ 015 Will Be the Last Time Tina Fey and Amy Poehler Host the Golden Globes]. Retrieved 15 December 2014. The nominations were announced on December 11, 2014 by [[Kate Beckinsale]], [[Peter Krause]], [[Paula Patton]] and [[Jeremy Piven]].[http://www.dickclark.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/12/72nd-Annual-Golden-Globes-Nominations.pdf 72ND ANNUAL GOLDEN GLOBE AWARDS NOMINEES ANNOUNCED] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170227021100/https://www.dickclark.com/wp-content/uploads/2014/12/72nd-Annual-Golden-Globes-Nominations.pdf |date=2017-02-27 }}. Retrieved 11 December 2014.{{cite web|url = https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/films/news/golden-globes-2015-list-of-nominees-in-full-9918054.html|title=Golden Globes 2015: List of nominations in full as Birdman leads with seven nods|date=11 December 2014|work=The Independent|access-date=11 December 2014}}{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/dec/11/golden-globes-birdman-boyhood-nominations|title=Golden Globe Awards: Boyhood, The Imitation Game and Birdman lead nominations|date=11 December 2014|work=Guardian |access-date=11 December 2014}} ''[[The Affair (TV series)|The Affair]]'', ''[[Birdman (film)|Birdman]]'', ''[[Boyhood (2014 film)|Boyhood]]'', ''[[Fargo (TV series)|Fargo]]'', ''[[The Theory of Everything (2014 film)|The Theory of Everything]]'', and ''[[Transparent (TV series)|Transparent]]'' were among the films and television shows that received multiple awards.{{cite web |url=http://www.cnn.com/2015/01/11/showbiz/feat-2015-golden-globes-winners-list/ |title=2015 Golden Globe Awards: Winners List |work=CNN |date=January 11, 2015 |access-date=January 11, 2015}} ==Winners and nominees== [[File:Eddie Redmayne & Aisha Tyler (43718217081) Cropped.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Eddie Redmayne]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Julianne Moore Cannes 2016 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Julianne Moore]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama winner]] [[File:Michael Keaton by Gage Skidmore.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Michael Keaton]], Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:Amy Adams UK Nocturnal Animals Premiere (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Amy Adams]], Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy winner]] [[File:JK Simmons 2009.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[J. K. Simmons]], Best Supporting Actor winner]] [[File:Patricia Arquette in 2011 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Patricia Arquette]], Best Supporting Actress winner]] [[File:Kevin Spacey, May 2013.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Kevin Spacey]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Ruth Wilson May 2015 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Ruth Wilson]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Drama winner]] [[File:Jeffrey Tambor June 2015.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Jeffrey Tambor]], Best Actor in a Television Series – Comedy or Musical winner]] [[File:Gina Rodriguez at 2014 PaleyFest.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Gina Rodriguez]], Best Actress in a Television Series – Comedy or Musical winner]] [[File:BillyBobThorntonHWOFFeb2012crop.JPG|right|thumb|150px|[[Billy Bob Thornton]], Best Actor in a Miniseries or Television Film winner]] [[File:Maggie Gyllenhaal Berlinale 2017.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Maggie Gyllenhaal]], Best Actress in a Miniseries or Television Film winner]] [[File:Matt Bomer Comic-con by Gage Skidmore.jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Matt Bomer]], Best Supporting Actor in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] [[File:Joanne Froggatt May 3, 2014 (cropped).jpg|right|thumb|150px|[[Joanne Froggatt]], Best Supporting Actress in a Series, Miniseries, or Television Film winner]] These are the nominees for the 72nd Golden Globe Awards. Film * [[Colin Firth]] introduced ''The Imitation Game'' * [[Jane Fonda]] and [[Lily Tomlin]] with Best Actor in a Television Series – Comedy or Musical * [[Harrison Ford]] with Best Director – Motion Picture * [[Ricky Gervais]] with Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy * [[Bill Hader]] and [[Kristen Wiig]] with Best Screenplay * [[Kevin Hart]] and [[Salma Hayek]] with intro of Miss Golden Globe and Best Animated Feature Film * [[Katie Holmes]] and [[Seth Meyers]] with Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film * [[Kate Hudson]] introduced ''Into the Woods'' * [[Jared Leto]] with Best Supporting Actress – Motion Picture * [[Adam Levine]] and [[Paul Rudd]] with Best Television Series – Drama * [[Jennifer Lopez]] and [[Jeremy Renner]] with Best Miniseries or Television Film and Best Actor – Miniseries or Television Film * [[Melissa McCarthy]] introduced ''St. Vincent'' * [[Matthew McConaughey]] with Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama * [[Sienna Miller]] and [[Vince Vaughn]] with Best Original Score * [[Clive Owen]] introduced ''The Theory of Everything'' * [[Gwyneth Paltrow]] with Best Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama * [[Prince (musician)|Prince]] with Best Original Song * [[Meryl Streep]] with Best Motion Picture – Drama * [[Channing Tatum]] introduced ''Foxcatcher'' * [[Naomi Watts]] introduced ''Birdman'' * [[Owen Wilson]] introduced ''The Grand Budapest Hotel'' * [[Oprah Winfrey]] introduced ''Selma'' * [[Catherine Zeta-Jones]] introduced ''Pride'' {{div col end}} ==Reception== The show received mixed to positive reviews, with critics praising hosts Tina Fey and Amy Poehler's jokes as well as the multitude of political commentary in speeches. However, the ceremony's lack of energy and adherence to routine faced negative criticism. Sean O'Neal of ''The A.V. Club'' lamented Fey and Poehler's previous announcement that this would be their final year hosting—"Hosts Tina Fey and Amy Poehler reminded everyone why they will be sorely missed next year"O'Neal, Sean. [https://www.avclub.com/article/boys-virgins-and-internet-win-big-golden-globes-213723 "Boys, virgins, and the Internet win big at the Golden Globes"], ''The A.V. Club'', 12 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015.—which presenter Meryl Streep echoed as she introduced the "Best Motion Picture—Drama" category.Gerard, Jeremy. [https://deadline.com/2015/01/golden-globes-review-2015-golden-globe-awards-1201347357/ "Amy Poehler & Tina Fey Leave The Bar High For Golden Globes Show – Review"], ''Deadline'', 11 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015. Jethro Nededog of ''The Wrap'' was among many reviewers to praise Fey and Poehler, though he wished they had appeared more often throughout the ceremony, claiming, "the fun was front-loaded".Nededog, Jethro. [https://www.thewrap.com/golden-globe-awards-review-checking-boxes-doesnt-make-for-an-entertaining-show/ "Golden Globe Awards Review: Checking Boxes Doesn't Make for an Entertaining Show"], ''The Wrap'', 11 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015. Comedian [[Margaret Cho]] appeared at several instances as a disapproving North Korean film critic, parodying North Korea's reaction and condemnation of the film ''[[The Interview]]'' two months earlier. Cho had previously played Kim Jong-Il and Kim Jong-Un on Fey's comedy series ''[[30 Rock]]'', the latter role garnering Cho a [[Primetime Emmy Award]] nomination for [[Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Guest Actress in a Comedy Series|Outstanding Guest Actress in a Comedy Series]].[http://www.emmys.com/bios/margaret-cho Margaret Cho], ''[[Emmys.com]]''. Retrieved 17 January 2015. Some reviewers enjoyed the recurring gag and Cho's skewering of [[Golden Globe Award for Best Television Series – Musical or Comedy|Best Television Series – Musical or Comedy]] nominee ''[[Orange Is the New Black]]''{{'}}s placement in the "Comedy" category. Others found Cho's appearances "interminable". Of note during the ceremony were overt references to oppressed populations and current political events in several victors' speeches.Poniewozik, James. [https://time.com/3663547/golden-globes-2015-review-fey-poehler-cosby-charlie-hebdo/ "Review: From Cosby to Charlie, This Golden Globes Had Something to Say"], ''Time'', 12 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015. [[Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress – Series, Miniseries or Television Film|Best Supporting Actress – Series, Miniseries or Television Film]] winner [[Joanne Froggatt]], referencing her ''[[Downton Abbey]]'' character [[List of Downton Abbey characters#Anna Bates|Anna Bates]]'s experience with [[sexual assault]], addressed real-world survivors of sexual abuse and rape. [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actor – Television Series Musical or Comedy|Best Actor – Television Series Musical or Comedy]] winner Jeffrey Tambor thanked the [[transgender]] community and ''[[Transparent (TV series)|Transparent]]'' creator [[Jill Soloway]] dedicated her [[Golden Globe Award for Best Television Series – Musical or Comedy|award]] to recent trans suicide victim [[Death of Leelah Alcorn|Leelah Alcorn]]. [[Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song|Best Original Song]] winner [[Common (rapper)|Common]] alluded to the [[Ferguson, Missouri]] police's [[shooting of Michael Brown]] as well as to the [[2014 NYPD officer killings]] by armed civilians. [[Golden Globe Award for Best Actress – Television Series Musical or Comedy|Best Actress – Television Series Musical or Comedy]] winner [[Gina Rodriguez]] mentioned her programs's relevance to [[Latino American|Latino]] viewers, and [[Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film|Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film]] winner [[Matt Bomer]] expressed solidarity and remorse for the [[LGBT]] victims of the [[AIDS]] crisis.Lowry, Brian. [https://variety.com/2015/tv/reviews/golden-globes-review-2015-golden-globe-awards-nbc-1201396928/ "TV Review: "The Golden Globe Awards"], ''Variety'', 11 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015. ===Ratings=== The ceremony averaged a Nielsen 12.6 rating/19 shareKinneally, Tim and Tony Maglio. [https://www.thewrap.com/ratings-golden-globe-awards-dip-from-2014s-10-year-high/ "Ratings: Golden Globe Awards Dip From Last Year (Updated)"], ''The Wrap'', 12 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015. and was watched by 19.3 million viewers. This rating was an eight percent decline from the [[71st Golden Globe Awards|previous ceremony's]] viewership of 20.9 million, the highest in a decade.[https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-30793677 "Golden Globe ratings dip after 2014 high"], ''BBC News'', 13 January 2015. Retrieved 17 January 2015. ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== * {{official website|http://www.goldenglobes.org/}} * {{IMDb title|3270224}} * {{Rotten Tomatoes TV|golden_globes/s72}} {{Golden Globe Awards Chron}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Golden Globe Awards}} [[Category:2014 film awards]] [[Category:2014 television awards]] [[Category:Golden Globe Awards ceremonies|*072]] [[Category:2014 awards in the United States|Golden Globe]] [[Category:January 2015 in the United States]]
